p_question-9587.ja:
以下は、留学生の作文である。


箸

ハーバート マーティン

　私は箸を使うのが苦手でした。大好きなラーメンもフォークで食べていました。でも、日本のラーメン屋には箸[19]ないことが多く、食べるのが大変でした。

　日本に来て２か月ぐらいたったころ、箸の専門店を見つけました。店に入ると、長さや太さ、材料や色の違う箸が何百種類もありました。その数に驚いていたら、店の人に、「どれでも自由に持ってみてください。」と言われました。 [20]いくつか実際に持ってみました。その中に持ちやすい箸がありました。

　それまで、箸を使うのはめんどうくさいと思っていましたが、そのときは、「これで練習したら、箸が上手に使えるようになるかもしれない」と思いました。私はその箸を買って帰りました。

　それから毎日、家でその箸を[21]。

　１か月ぐらいたって慣れたところ、ラーメンを食べに行きました。すると、[22]箸も楽に使えるようになっていました。

　私はずっと、箸をうまく使うことはあきらめていました。うまく使えるようになったのは、専門店で買った[23]。今は前よりもラーメン屋に行くのが楽しみになりました。

The following is an essay written by an international student.


Chopsticks

Herbert Martin

I was not good at using chopsticks. I even ate my favorite ramen with a fork. However, many ramen shops in Japan do not have forks [19], so it was difficult to eat.

About two months after coming to Japan, I found a specialty shop for chopsticks. When I entered the store, there were hundreds of chopsticks of different lengths, thicknesses, materials, and colors. While I was surprised by the number, a clerk told me, “Please feel free to hold any of them.” [20] I actually picked up several pairs. Among them, there was one that was easy to hold.

Until then, I thought using chopsticks was troublesome, but at that moment, I thought, “If I practice with these, I might be able to use chopsticks well.” I bought that pair and went home.

After that, every day at home, I [21] with those chopsticks.

About a month later, when I had gotten used to them, I went to eat ramen. Then, [22] I was able to use the chopsticks easily.

I had always given up on being able to use chopsticks well. The reason I became able to use them skillfully was [23] the chopsticks I bought at the specialty shop. Now I enjoy going to ramen shops more than before.

p_question-9592.ja:
（１）

これはカレンさんが田中先生に書いた手紙である。


田中先生

　こぶさたしております。お元気ですか。

　帰国してから、もう半年が過ぎましたが、日本語クラスで勉強していたころのことを、いつもなつかしく思い出しています。

　7月から大学が休みになるので、2週間ぐらい、姉と日本を旅行します。京都や広島を回る予定ですが、13日から15日の間は東京にいます。東京にいる間に大学に伺って、ぜひ先生にお目にかかりたいと思っています。もしご都合のいい日がありましたら、メールでお返事をいただけないでしょうか。

　よろしくお願いいたします。

4月10日カレン・コルベイKaren__beil@xxmail.com

(1)

This is a letter written by Karen to Professor Tanaka.


Professor Tanaka

It has been a while. How have you been?

It has already been half a year since I returned to my country, but I often fondly remember the time when I studied in your Japanese class.

From July, my university will be on vacation, so my sister and I will travel around Japan for about two weeks. We plan to visit Kyoto and Hiroshima, and we’ll be in Tokyo from the 13th to the 15th. While in Tokyo, I would love to visit the university and see you. If there’s a convenient day for you, could you please reply to me by email?

Thank you very much.

April 10
Karen Korubei
Karen__beil@xxmail.com

p_question-9593.ja:
（２）

　私にとって、部屋が片付いていないことほどストレスのたまることはない。でも、夫も子供も「片付けられない人」で、服は脱いだまま、おもちゃは出したままで全く平気だ。文句を言うと、入れる場所が少なすぎるとか、すぐ使うからとか、いろいろなことを言う。片付けは片付けが得意な人がやればいいと言われた時は、本当に嫌になった。使い終わったら、それをあった所に戻すだけでいいのに、なぜこんな簡単なことができないのだろう。

(2)

Nothing stresses me out more than having a messy room. However, my husband and children are the type of people who “can’t clean up,” and they don’t mind leaving their clothes lying around or toys out everywhere. When I complain, they say things like there isn’t enough storage space or that they’ll use it soon. When my husband said that tidying up should be done by the one who’s good at it, I really got fed up. All they have to do is put things back where they belong after using them — why can’t they do such a simple thing?

p_question-9594.ja:
（３）

　キャッシュカードなどを作る時に必要な暗証番号を決めるのは意外に難しい。電話番号などの自分に関係のある数字を使えば忘れにくいが、他人に知られる危険がある。でも、自分に関係のない数字や、不規則に並んだ数字にすると忘れやすい。では、自分に関係のある数字に単純な数字を足すのはどうだろうか。例えば、誕生日の数字「1025」に「1111」を足した「2136」を暗証番号にするのだ。これなら、他人にはわかりにくく、自分には思い出しやすくてよいだろう。

(3)

It’s surprisingly difficult to decide on a PIN when making a cash card or something similar. Using numbers related to yourself, like your phone number, makes them easy to remember but risky because others might figure them out. However, using unrelated or random numbers makes them easy to forget. So how about adding a simple number to one related to yourself? For example, if your birthday is “1025,” you could add “1111” to make “2136” as your PIN. This way, others won’t easily guess it, and it’ll still be easy for you to remember.

p_question-9595.ja:
（４）

これは野村課長が、同じ会社の内田さんに書いたメールである。



内田さん

　今、池原駅に向かう電車の中にいます。信号故障の影響で、電車が止まってしまいました。車内アナウンスによると、動きだすまで30分くらいかかるそうです。

　オオタ工業を訪問する前に、駅前の喫茶店で最終確認をする約束でしたが、その時間はなさそうです。私は駅から直接向かうので、先にオオタ工業に行って、入口前で待っていてください。もし時間があれば、そこで簡単に最終確認をしましょう。

　訪問する時間に間に合わない場合は、また連絡します。

　よろしくお願いします。

野村

(4)

This is an email written by Section Chief Nomura to Mr. Uchida, who works at the same company.


Mr. Uchida

I am currently on the train heading to Ikehara Station. Due to a signal malfunction, the train has stopped. According to the announcement, it will take about 30 minutes before it starts moving again.

We were supposed to do a final check at the café in front of the station before visiting Oota Industries, but it seems that won’t be possible. I’ll go straight from the station, so please go ahead to Oota Industries first and wait in front of the entrance. If we have time, let’s do a quick final check there.

If it looks like I won’t make it in time for the visit, I’ll contact you again.

Thank you.

Nomura

p_question-9596.ja:
（１）

　秋になり、文房具屋に来年の手帳が並ぶようになった。一般的に、手帳を選ぶ場合に重要なのは、書きやすさ、見やすさだろう。大きさも大切な点だ。だが、私がいちばん大切にしているのは、表紙だ。絵や柄で選ぶ人もいるが、私は色で選ぶ。

　いつも手帳を持ち歩く私は、手帳の色に影響を受けていると感じることがある。予定を書いたり、友達と会う場所を確認したりするため、一日に何度もかばんから取り出すが、そのたびに表紙の色が目に入るからだ。それで、この数年は元気が出るように派手な表紙のものを選んできた。今使っているのも、明るいオレンジ色だ。考えてみると、手帳は家族や親友より、一緒にいる時間も長いのだ。

　でも、今年は手帳選びに時間がかかっている。30歳になり、もっと落ち着いた気持ちで仕事をしたいと思うようになってきたのだ。そうは言っても、ストレスのたまる仕事なので、やはりこのまま元気の出る赤や黄か、気分の落ち着く黒や茶か、なかなか決められないでいる。

(1)

As autumn comes, next year’s planners start to appear in stationery stores. Generally, when choosing a planner, the important things are how easy it is to write in and how easy it is to read. The size is also an important factor. However, what I value most is the cover. Some people choose by design or pattern, but I choose by color.

Because I always carry my planner around, I sometimes feel influenced by its color. I take it out of my bag many times a day to write down plans or check where I’m meeting friends, and every time, the color of the cover catches my eye. That’s why, for the past few years, I’ve chosen bright covers that lift my mood. The one I’m using now is a cheerful orange. Come to think of it, I spend more time with my planner than even with my family or close friends.

However, this year, it’s taking me a long time to choose one. I’ve turned 30, and I’ve started wanting to work with a calmer mindset. Still, since my job is stressful, I can’t decide whether to go with a bright, energizing color like red or yellow, or a calm one like black or brown.

p_question-9599.ja:
（２）

「負けないで。君は一人じゃない。」

　今朝、車で駅に向かっている時、赤信号で止まっていると、ラジオから懐かしい曲が流れてきました。ずっと聞いていたかった曲です。隣に座る高校生の娘に「これ、お母さんの若いころの応援歌だったのよ。」と言ってしばらく聞いていると、涙が出そうになって困りました。①「お母さん、青。」という娘の声で、慌ててアクセルを踏んだのですが、娘に笑われてしまいました。

　20代の初め、会社に勤め始めてすぐのころの私は、毎日失敗ばかりして泣いていました。よくしかられ、人間関係にも悩んでいました。そんな時、いつも②この曲を聞いていたのです。「負けないで。君は一人じゃない。」という言葉に慰められ、この曲を聞くと、頑張ろうという気持ちになりました。

　あれからもう20年です。泣きたくなるような苦しいことも、いつのまにかなくなりました。③今はきっと幸せなのだと思います。今日、本当に久しぶりにこの曲を聞いたのですから。

(2)

“Don’t give up. You’re not alone.”

This morning, while driving to the station, I stopped at a red light, and a nostalgic song started playing on the radio. It was a song I had wanted to hear again for a long time. I told my high school daughter sitting next to me, “This was my encouragement song when I was young.” As we listened for a while, I almost started to cry. 
① “Mom, it’s green.” said my daughter, and I hurriedly stepped on the gas, which made her laugh.

In my early twenties, just after I started working at a company, I failed a lot and cried every day. I was often scolded and struggled with relationships. At such times, I always listened to ② this song. The words “Don’t give up. You’re not alone.” encouraged me, and whenever I listened to it, I felt motivated to keep going.

It’s been twenty years since then. The hardships that used to make me want to cry have somehow disappeared. ③ I must be happy now, I think. After all, I heard this song again today for the first time in a long while.

p_question-9602.ja:
川村さんは都会のマンションに住んでいる。野菜や花を育てることが好きなのだが、マンションのベランダでは十分に楽しむことができない。広い庭のある家で、野菜や花を育てることが、川村さんの長い間の夢だった。

　川村さんは、今、広い「庭」で野菜や花を育てている。近くに「みんなの庭」ができたのだ。「みんなの庭」は、市の土地を市民が借りて、共同の「庭」を作っていくものである。利用する人たちが話し合って、みんなで野菜や花を育てて楽しむ。

「みんなの庭」で野菜や花を作るようになって、川村さんは友達がたくさんできたそうだ。それまでは、近所の人にあいさつはしても、なかなか友達になるのは難しかった。しかし、①「みんなの庭」では違う。同じようにこの庭を利用して野菜や花を作っている人に、「きれいに咲きましたね。」とか「りっぱなトマトになりましたね。」と話しかけたりする。また、普段あまり付き合うことがない人とお茶を飲んだり、おしゃべりをしたりすることも、ここなら自然にできるそうだ。都会では少なくなった近所の人との交流の機会が生まれているのだ。

　川村さんたちは、「みんなの庭」で近所の人と付き合っているうちに、子どもの教育や町の安全など、生活のこともいろいろ話すようになった。このような付き合いが続いていけば、将来は、自分たちの町をもっと住みやすくしていこうという話し合いができるようになるかもしれない。川村さんは、「みんなの庭」で汗を流しながら、②そう感じている。

Mr. Kawamura lives in a city apartment. He loves growing vegetables and flowers, but he couldn’t fully enjoy it on his apartment balcony. His long-held dream was to live in a house with a large garden where he could grow vegetables and flowers.

Now, Mr. Kawamura grows vegetables and flowers in a large “garden.” A “community garden” has been created nearby. This “community garden” is a shared space where citizens rent land from the city and work together to grow vegetables and flowers. The people who use it discuss and enjoy growing plants together.

Since he started gardening in the “community garden,” Mr. Kawamura says he’s made many friends. Before, although he greeted his neighbors, it was difficult to actually become friends. However, ① “it’s different in the community garden.” He chats with people who are also growing vegetables and flowers there, saying things like “Your flowers bloomed beautifully” or “That’s a fine tomato.” He also naturally ends up drinking tea or chatting with people he normally wouldn’t interact with. This has created opportunities for neighborly connections that have become rare in urban life.

As Mr. Kawamura and others spent time with neighbors in the “community garden,” they began discussing various aspects of daily life, such as children’s education and neighborhood safety. If such relationships continue, they might someday start discussions about making their town a better place to live. While working hard in the garden, Mr. Kawamura ② feels that way.

questions.stem: 塩は台所にありました。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 3: しお is correct.

Question Translation
「塩は台所にありました。」
→ "The salt was in the kitchen."

Explanation
The kanji 「塩」 is read **しお**, meaning *salt*. In this sentence it names the item’s location.

Other Options
1. あぶら — 油 (*oil*); different word/kanji.
2. まめ — 豆 (*beans*); not 「塩」.
4. こめ — 米 (*rice*); not 「塩」.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 「塩(しお)」 — 소금. 「台所(だいどころ)にある」와 자연스럽게 호응한다.
오답: 1 あぶら는 「油」, 2 まめ는 「豆」, 4 こめ는 「米」로 모두 다른 한자·단어.
포인트: 塩=しお, 음독은 エン(塩分).

questions.stem: 彼は犬に命令した。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 4: めいれい is correct.

Question Translation
「彼は犬に命令した。」
→ "He gave the dog an order."

Explanation
「命令」 is read **めいれい** and means *order/command*. It correctly matches the verb した.

Other Options
1. めれん — not a word/reading for 命令.
2. めいれん — incorrect reading; 命令 is not read this way.
3. めれい — not a standard reading of 命令.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 「命令(めいれい)」 — 명령. 「した」와 결합해 자연스럽다.
오답: 1 めれん·2 めいれん·3 めれい는 모두 존재하지 않는 음. 장음 「めい」+「れい」 결합에 주의.
포인트: 命=メイ, 令=レイ. 둘 다 장음.

questions.stem: 手紙には恋しい人を思う気持ちが書かれていた。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 1: こいしい is correct.

Question Translation
「手紙には恋しい人を思う気持ちが書かれていた。」
→ "The letter described feelings of longing for a beloved person."

Explanation
「恋しい」 is read **こいしい** and means *dear/longed-for*, fitting the context of thinking of someone.

Other Options
2. したしい — 親しい (*intimate/close*); different word/kanji.
3. なつかしい — (*nostalgic*); nuance differs from 恋しい.
4. やさしい — 優しい (*kind/gentle*); unrelated meaning.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 「恋しい(こいしい)」 — 그립다, 사랑스럽다. 「思う気持ち」와 호응.
오답: 2 したしい는 「親しい」(친하다), 3 なつかしい는 「懐かしい」(그립다, 옛 추억), 4 やさしい는 「優しい」(상냥하다)로 한자가 다름.
포인트: 恋=コイ, 訓は こい(しい).

questions.stem: 機械が故障した。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 2: きかい is correct.

Question Translation
「機械が故障した。」
→ "The machine broke down."

Explanation
「機械」 is read **きかい** meaning *machine*. It matches the breakdown context.

Other Options
1. ぎかい — 議会 (*assembly/parliament*).
3. きけい — 奇形 (*deformity*).
4. ぎけい — 義兄 (*older brother-in-law*).
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 「機械(きかい)」 — 기계. 「故障する」(고장 나다)와 잘 어울린다.
오답: 1 ぎかい는 「議会」(의회), 3 きけい는 「奇形」, 4 ぎけい는 「義兄」(처남·매부) — 발음이 비슷한 함정.
포인트: 機=キ, 械=カイ. 탁음·장음 없음.

questions.stem: 今月は休日が多かった。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 2: きゅうじつ is correct.

Question Translation
「今月は休日が多かった。」
→ "There were many days off this month."

Explanation
「休日」 is read **きゅうじつ**, meaning *day off/holiday (general)*.

Other Options
1. しゅくじつ — 祝日 (*public/national holiday*); different word.
3. きゅうにち — not a standard reading.
4. しゅくにち — not a standard word.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 「休日(きゅうじつ)」 — 휴일. 「今月は～が多かった」와 자연스럽다.
오답: 1 しゅくじつ는 「祝日」(국경일)로 단어가 다름. 3 きゅうにち·4 しゅくにち는 표준 음독이 아님.
포인트: 休=キュウ, 日=ジツ(음독).

questions.stem: この部分をよく見てください。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 3: ぶぶん is correct.

Question Translation
「この部分をよく見てください。」
→ "Please look carefully at this part."

Explanation
「部分」 is read **ぶぶん** meaning *part/section*.

Other Options
1. ぶふん — incorrect reading.
2. ぶうぶん — not a word.
4. ぶうふん — not a word.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 「部分(ぶぶん)」 — 부분.
오답: 1 ぶふん, 2 ぶうぶん, 4 ぶうふん은 모두 존재하지 않는 음. 「ぶ+ぶん」으로 장음·탁음 변화 없음.
포인트: 部=ブ, 分=ブン.

questions.stem: 池田さんは木村さんを疑っているようだ。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 4: うたがって is correct.

Question Translation
「池田さんは木村さんを疑っているようだ。」
→ "It seems Mr. Ikeda suspects Mr. Kimura."

Explanation
The te-form of 「疑う」 is **疑って（うたがって）**, meaning *to suspect/doubt*.

Other Options
1. しかって — 叱って (*scolding*).
2. こわがって — 怖がって (*being afraid*).
3. きらって — 嫌って (*disliking*).
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 「疑(うたが)って」 — 의심하다(て형). 「池田さんは木村さんを～」와 호응.
오답: 1 しかって는 「叱って」(꾸짖다), 2 こわがって는 「怖がって」(무서워하다), 3 きらって는 「嫌って」(싫어하다) — 모두 다른 동사.
포인트: 疑う=うたがう. 부정적 감정 동사를 한 번에 정리.

questions.stem: 中島さんは数学が得意らしい。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 1: とくい is correct.

Question Translation
「中島さんは数学が得意らしい。」
→ "It seems Mr. Nakajima is good at math."

Explanation
「得意」 is read **とくい**, meaning *strong at/skillful*. 

Other Options
2. とうい — not a standard reading.
3. どくい — incorrect voicing; not used.
4. どうい — 同意 (*agreement/consent*); different word.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 「得意(とくい)」 — 잘함, 자신 있음. 「数学が～」와 호응.
오답: 2 とうい·3 どくい는 없는 음. 4 どうい는 「同意」(동의)로 단어가 다름.
포인트: 得=トク, 意=イ. 「得意↔苦手(にがて)」.

questions.stem: 毎日パソコンを使うので、目のつかれが取れません。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 3 is correct. It means 「疲れ」 (tiredness/fatigue).

Question Translation
"Because I use a computer every day, the tiredness in my eyes won’t go away."

Explanation
The correct kanji for つかれ (tsukare) meaning *fatigue* is **疲れ**.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 「腐れ」 — (くされ) “rottenness,” “decay,” or “bad/rotten condition.”
2. 「病れ」 — “to be ill”; not used as a noun for eye fatigue.
4. 「痛れ」 — not a standard word; *pain* is 痛み.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
疲れ (noun) comes from the verb 疲れる (*to get tired*).
questions.expl_ko:

포인트: つかれる=疲れる. 신체·정신의 피로 모두 사용.

questions.stem: そんなになかないでください。
questions.expl:

Question Translation

Explanation
The verb *to cry (tears)* is **泣く**; negative request form is **泣かないで**.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 「涙かないで」 — ‘涙’ (tears) + かないで is incorrect; should be 泣かないで.
2. 「悲かないで」 — 悲しい is an adjective; not used with ～かないで.
3. 「冷かないで」 — not grammatical; ‘tease’ is 冷やかさないで.

포인트: 동사 「泣く」의 부정 의뢰형 「泣かないで(ください)」.

questions.stem: この交差点はうせつできません。

questions.stem: 最近、父は仕事が忙しいらしく、きたくが遅い日が多い。

questions.stem: 距離
opts: 東京から京都までの距離
オリンピックは４年に１回の距離
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 1 is correct.

Word Translation
距離（きょり） — distance (space between two points)

Correct Sentence Translation
"I checked how many kilometers the distance is from Tokyo to Kyoto."

Explanation
**距離** refers to spatial distance. "東京から京都までの距離" is a natural, common collocation.

Other Options
② ズボンの距離を少し短くしてもらった。
→ “I had the distance of my pants shortened.”
❌ Unnatural. Should be 丈（たけ） or 裾（すそ）.

③ オリンピックは４年に１回の距離で開かれる。
→ “The Olympics are held at a distance of once every four years.”
❌ Wrong word; use 間隔/周期.

④ 私と弟は５歳の距離があるので…
→ “There is a distance of five years between me and my brother…”
🔶 While metaphorical use exists, 年齢差/年の差 is natural. Marked as incorrect in this set.

Usage Note
距離 pairs with words like 移動距離, 走行距離, 距離感 (figurative). For time/age use 間隔／差.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 — 「東京から京都までの距離(きょり)」(도쿄에서 교토까지의 거리). 距離=공간적 거리.
오답: 2 ズボンの距離(바지 길이) → 長さ, 3 オリンピックは４年に１回の距離 → 間隔(かんかく), 4 私と弟は５歳の距離 → ５歳の差.
포인트: 距離는 두 지점 사이의 물리적·심리적 거리.

questions.stem: 活動
opts: 雪のせいで列車の活動
いろいろな国の文化を子どもたちに紹介する活動
走る前に足や腰の活動
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 3 is correct.

Word Translation
活動（かつどう） — activity, (organized) action

Correct Sentence Translation
"I took part in activities that introduce the cultures of various countries to children."

Explanation
**活動** fits organized programs or initiatives.

Other Options
① 雪のせいで列車の活動が止まっている。
→ “…the trains’ activity has stopped.”
❌ Use 運行/運転.

② このスーパーの活動は夜９時までです。
→ “…this supermarket’s activity is until 9 p.m.”
❌ Use 営業時間/営業時間は…まで.

④ 走る前に…の活動をすると…
→ “…do activity of legs/waist…”
❌ Use 準備運動/ストレッチ.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 — 「いろいろな国の文化を子どもたちに紹介する活動(かつどう)」. 活動=조직된 활동·운동.
오답: 1 列車の活動 → 運行(うんこう), 2 スーパーの活動 → 営業(えいぎょう), 4 足や腰の活動 → 運動·ストレッチ.
포인트: 活動은 단체·사업 등 조직적 행위에 쓰임.

questions.stem: 盛ん
opts: このテレビは古いから、故障が盛ん
弟は写真をとるのが盛ん
私の学校はスポーツが盛ん
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 4 is correct.

Word Translation
盛ん（さかん） — active, flourishing, popular/widely practiced

Correct Sentence Translation
"At my school, sports are active and everyone does various sports." (→ Sports are very popular at my school.)

Explanation
**盛ん** commonly describes areas where an activity is popular or vigorous (スポーツが盛ん, 貿易が盛ん).

Other Options
① 最近勉強が盛んだったので…
→ “My studying was flourishing recently…”
🔶 Grammatically possible but unnatural; use 一生懸命/熱心に.

② このテレビは…故障が盛んになってきた。
→ “…breakdowns became vigorous.”
❌ 不自然; use 故障が増えてきた.

③ 弟は写真をとるのが盛んで…
→ “My brother is flourishing at taking photos.”
🔶 Sounds odd; say 写真を撮るのが好き/熱心だ.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 「私の学校はスポーツが盛(さか)ん」(우리 학교는 스포츠가 활발하다).
오답: 1 勉強が盛ん → '공부' 자체엔 부자연(熱心), 2 故障が盛ん → 多い, 3 写真をとるのが盛ん → 好き/得意.
포인트: 「N(분야)が盛ん」=어느 분야가 왕성·번창. スポーツ·貿易·研究 등.

questions.stem: 区別
opts: 卒業したら、同級生たちは大学や就職など区別して進みます。
意味のよく似ていることばを区別して覚えるのは難しい。
石川さんにはずっと会っていないから、今会っても区別できないかもしれない。
私の国は、２本の大きな川でほかの国と区別されています。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 2 is correct.

Word Translation
区別（くべつ） — distinction; to tell apart / differentiate

Correct Sentence Translation
"It is difficult to memorize words with very similar meanings by distinguishing between them."

Explanation
**区別する** is the act of distinguishing one from another, which matches learning similar words.

Other Options
① 卒業したら…区別して進みます。
→ “After graduating, classmates will proceed distinguished into university or jobs.”
❌ Use それぞれ/別々に/進路が分かれる.

③ …今会っても区別できないかもしれない。
→ “…even if I meet him now, I might not be able to distinguish (him).”
🔶 For recognizing a person, **見分ける/見分けがつかない** is natural; 区別 sounds category-based.

④ 私の国は…ほかの国と区別されています。
→ “My country is distinguished from other countries by two big rivers.”
❌ Physical separation/borders use 区切られている/隔てられている, not 区別.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 「意味のよく似ていることばを区別(くべつ)して覚える」(의미가 비슷한 단어를 구별해서 외우다).
오답: 1 大学や就職など区別して進む → 分かれて, 3 区別できない → 見分(みわ)けられない, 4 ２本の川で区別される → 分けられている/隔(へだ)てられている.
포인트: 区別=비슷한 것 사이의 차이를 인지·분류.

questions.stem: 追いつく
opts: 棚の一番上の本を取りたいが、手が追いつかない
道が渋滞していて、会議の時間に追いつかなかった
５時間以上登って、ついに山の最も高い所に追いついた
妹より後に家を出たが、駅の近くで追いついた
questions.expl:
Correct Answer  
Answer 4 is correct.

Word Translation  
追いつく — to catch up (with); to keep up with

Correct Sentence Translation  
I left home after my sister, but I caught up with her near the station.

Explanation  
追いつく is used when you close a gap and catch up to someone/something that is ahead, or metaphorically when you keep up with work, demand, or progress. The natural pattern is AがBに追いつく. In Answer 4, you physically catch up to your sister who left earlier, so it fits perfectly.

Other Options

① 棚の一番上の本を取りたいが、手が追いつかない。  
→ I want to get the book on the top shelf, but my hand can’t catch up.  
❌ Incorrect Usage  
For “can’t reach,” Japanese uses 手が届かない. 手が追いつかない is used for not keeping up with workload/demand (e.g., 注文が多すぎて手が追いつかない), not for physical reach.

② 道が渋滞していて、会議の時間に追いつかなかった。  
→ The road was jammed, so I couldn’t catch up to the meeting time.  
❌ Incorrect Usage  
To say you weren’t in time, use 間に合わなかった. 〜の時間に追いつく is unnatural. You can say 予定に/スケジュールに追いつかない when you can’t keep up with pace, but not for arriving late to a meeting.

③ ５時間以上登って、ついに山の最も高い所に追いついた。  
→ After climbing for over five hours, we finally caught up to the highest point of the mountain.  
❌ Incorrect Usage  
追いつく requires a moving or progressing target. A place/peak doesn’t “move,” so use 到達した, たどり着いた, or 登頂した.

④ 妹より後に家を出たが、駅の近くで追いついた。  
→ I left later than my sister, but I caught up with her near the station.  
✅ Natural Usage  
This is the standard “catch up to someone” use. You could also say 妹に追いついた for clarity.

Usage Note  
- Pattern: AがBに追いつく (A catches up with B).  
- Common collocations: 人に追いつく, 仕事に追いつく, 授業に追いつく, 需要に供給が追いつかない.  
- Contrast: 間に合う (be in time), 届く (reach physically), 追いかける (chase), 追い越す (overtake).
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 「妹より後に家を出たが、駅の近くで追(お)いついた」(여동생보다 늦게 나갔지만 역 근처에서 따라잡았다).
오답: 1 手が追いつかない → 届(とど)かない, 2 会議の時間に追いつかなかった → 間に合わなかった, 3 山の最も高い所に追いついた → たどり着いた.
포인트: 追いつく=앞선 대상을 따라잡다(거리·진도).

questions.stem: 今年は夏休みに国へ帰りません。日本で夏休み（　　　）過ごすのは今年が初めてです。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 4: を is correct. It means the direct object marker used with 過ごす (“to spend [time]”).

Question Translation
“I won’t go back to my country for summer vacation this year. This is my first year to spend the summer vacation in Japan.”

Explanation
The verb ～を過ごす requires を to mark the time/period spent: 夏休み**を**過ごす.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. と — “with/and; as” → 夏休みと過ごす sounds like “spend with summer vacation,” unnatural.
2. が — subject marker → 夏休みが過ごす is ungrammatical (夏休み is not the subject doing the spending).
3. へ — “to/toward” (direction) → 夏休みへ過ごす is not used; 過ごす doesn’t take へ.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
Collocation: 時間／休み／休日＋を＋過ごす = “spend (time/holiday).”
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 「を」 — 직접 목적어 조사. 「夏休みを過(す)ごす」(여름방학을 보내다)가 정형.
오답: 1 と(인용·동반), 2 が(주격·대상), 3 へ(방향)는 「過ごす」와 결합하지 않음.
포인트: 「(기간)を過ごす」가 표준 결합.

questions.stem: この美術館では、10年前から外国人旅行者（　　　）チケットの割引サービスを行っている。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 1: に対して is correct. It means “toward/for (a target).”

Question Translation
“At this museum, they have been offering a ticket discount service for foreign tourists since ten years ago.”

Explanation
～に対して marks the target/recipient of an action or policy: 外国人旅行者**に対して**割引サービスを行う.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
2. に比べて — “compared with” → Not a comparison; it marks contrast, not a target.
3. のことで — “about/regarding” → Sounds like topic explanation, not who receives the discount.
4. のほかに — “in addition to” → Would mean “besides X,” which changes the meaning.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
に対して often pairs with 行う／発表する／アンケートを取る to indicate the target group.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 「に対(たい)して」 — ~에 대하여(대상·상대).
오답: 2 「に比べて」(~에 비해 — 비교), 3 「のことで」(~의 일로), 4 「のほかに」(~외에)는 대상·정책 수혜자를 가리키는 의미가 없음.
포인트: 「Nに対して+행위·태도」 정형.

questions.stem: 今朝（　　　）早起きをしてお弁当を作ったのに、持ってくるのを忘れてしまった。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 2: せっかく is correct. It means “with effort/after going to the trouble.”

Question Translation
“Even though I got up early this morning on purpose and made a bento, I ended up forgetting to bring it.”

Explanation
せっかく emphasizes effort or a favorable opportunity that was wasted; fits the contrast with のに.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. ぜったい — “absolutely/definitely” → Doesn’t express wasted effort.
3. けっして — “never/by no means” → Takes a negative verb; doesn’t fit meaning.
4. まったく — “completely” → Adverb of degree; doesn’t convey the contrast.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
Pattern: せっかく～のに／だのに = “even though (after going to the trouble) …”.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 「せっかく」 — 모처럼, 일부러. 「のに」와 호응해 헛수고가 됨을 강조.
오답: 1 「ぜったい」(절대로), 3 「けっして+ない」(결코), 4 「まったく」(전혀)는 노력·기회의 뉘앙스가 없음.
포인트: 「せっかく～のに」=모처럼 ~했는데도.

questions.stem: 健康でいるためには、食事に（　　　）、運動もしたほうがいいことはわかっているが、忙しくてなかなか運動する時間がとれない。
opts: 気をつけるだけではなく
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 4: 気をつけるだけではなく is correct. It means “not only be careful about (diet) but also …”.

Question Translation
“To stay healthy, I know that not only should I pay attention to my diet, but I should also exercise; however, I’m busy and can hardly find time to exercise.”

Explanation
だけではなく links two obligations: 食事に気をつける**だけではなく**、運動もしたほうがいい.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 気をつけることで — “by paying attention” → Doesn’t coordinate with 運動も; wrong relation.
2. 気をつけるかどうか — “whether to pay attention” → Not the intended meaning.
3. 気をつけるのだから — “since [I] pay attention” → Logical cause is odd; doesn’t pair with もしたほうがいい.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
だけでなく／だけではなく A、B = “not only A but also B.”
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 「気をつけるだけではなく」 — ~에 주의할 뿐 아니라.
오답: 1 「気をつけることで」(주의함으로써)는 수단, 2 「～かどうか」(여부)는 의문, 3 「～のだから」(~이니까)는 이유 — 모두 '추가'의 뉘앙스 없음.
포인트: 「A だけではなくBも」=A뿐 아니라 B도. 추가·열거.

questions.stem:
Ａ「ねえ、最近、娘がピアノを習いたいと言ってるから、（　　　）と思ってるんだけど、４歳じゃまだ早いと思う？」
Ｂ「ううん、別に早くないと思うよ。習わせてみたらいいんじゃない？」
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 2: 習わせようか is correct. It means “Shall I have (her) take lessons?” (seeking advice).

Question Translation
“A: Hey, lately our daughter says she wants to learn piano, so I’m thinking of ‘maybe having her start’, but do you think four years old is still too early?
B: No, I don’t think it’s too early. Why not let her try?”

Explanation
Volitional + か expresses consulting/sounding someone out: 習わせ**ようか**と思っている.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 習わせるだろうか — “I wonder if I will make her learn” → Introspective doubt, not a proposal.
3. 習わせているのだ — “(We) are making her learn (now)” → States a fact; contradicts context.
4. 習わせるつもりだ — “I intend to make her learn” → Decision already made; doesn’t ask for opinion.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
～ようかと思う is common for soft proposals, inviting the listener’s view.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 「習(なら)わせようか」 — 배우게 할까. 의향형으로 상의·제안.
오답: 1 「だろうか」(추측의문), 3 「ているのだ」(현재 진행), 4 「つもりだ」(단정적 의도)는 상대에게 의견을 구하는 뉘앙스가 약함.
포인트: 「(さ)せようか」+상대에게 묻기=권유·상의.

questions.stem: 自分の会社をつくったと言うとよく驚かれるが、会社をつくることは必ずしも（　　　）。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 3: 難しいことではない is correct. It means “is not necessarily difficult.”

Question Translation
“When I say I started my own company, people are often surprised, but starting a company is not necessarily difficult.”

Explanation
必ずしも pairs with a negative: 必ずしも～**ない**. 「難しいことではない」 fits the pattern.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 難しいに違いない — “must be difficult” → Opposite meaning.
2. 難しいはずがない — “can’t be difficult” → Too strong and doesn’t match 必ずしも pattern.
4. 難しいかもしれない — “might be difficult” → Doesn’t take 必ずしも; nuance differs.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
必ずしも A(だ) とは限らない／A とは言えない／A ではない = “not necessarily A.”
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 「難(むずか)しいことではない」 — 어려운 일은 아니다.
오답: 1 「に違いない」(틀림없다), 2 「はずがない」(~할 리 없다), 4 「かもしれない」(~일지도 모른다)는 「必ずしも」와 호응하지 않음.
포인트: 「必ずしも～ない」(반드시 ~인 것은 아니다)는 부분 부정 정형.

questions.stem:
（レストランで）
客「ランチセットを一つお願いします。」
店員「ＡセットとＢセットのどちらに（　　　）か。」
客「あ、Ａセットをお願いします。」
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 3: なさいます is correct. It’s the honorific “do/choose,” appropriate for service speech.

Question Translation
“(At a restaurant)
Customer: One lunch set, please.
Staff: Which would you like, A set or B set?
Customer: Ah, I’ll have A set.”

Explanation
どちらに**なさいます**か is a standard polite way to ask a customer’s choice.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 食べられます — “can eat” → Asks about ability, not choice.
2. 差し上げます — “I humbly give” → Speaker giving something; wrong pattern.
4. ございます — “there is/are (polite)” → Not used to ask for selection.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
Service keigo: いかが**なさいます**か／どちらに**なさいます**か = “What would you like?”
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 「なさいます」 — 「する」의 존경어. 손님에게 선택을 묻는 정중 표현.
오답: 1 「食べられます」는 가능·존경(혼동 가능하지만 メニュー 선택은 「なさる」), 2 「差し上げます」(드리다 — 점원이 손님에게 안 씀), 4 「ございます」(있다·있습니다).
포인트: 「どちらになさいますか」=어느 쪽으로 하시겠습니까. 접객 정형.

questions.stem:
（会社で）
田中「すみません、森さん。私、先週のセミナー、出張で出られなかったので、資料を（　　　）。」
森「あ、いいですよ。これです。どうぞ。」
opts: コピーさせるでしょうか
コピーさせていただけませんか
コピーしたらいかがですか
コピーするのではないでしょうか
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 2: コピーさせていただけませんか is correct. It means “Could you let me make a copy?” (polite request for permission).

Question Translation
“(At the office)
Tanaka: Excuse me, Ms. Mori. I couldn’t attend last week’s seminar because I was on a business trip, so could I make a copy of the materials?
Mori: Oh, sure. Here you are.”

Explanation
～させていただけませんか politely asks to be allowed to do something.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. コピーさせるでしょうか — “Will you make (someone) copy?” → Wrong subject/meaning.
3. コピーしたらいかがですか — “How about copying (them)?” → Suggests to the listener, not asking permission for oneself.
4. コピーするのではないでしょうか — “Isn’t it that (I) will copy?” → Odd, speculative tone.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
自分の行為の許可を丁寧に依頼: ～させていただけませんか／～させていただけますか.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 「コピーさせていただけませんか」 — 복사해도 될까요(겸손한 허가 요청).
오답: 1 「させるでしょうか」는 의미 불명, 3 「したらいかがですか」(~하면 어떨까요 — 권유), 4 「するのではないでしょうか」(~인 것 아닐까요 — 추측).
포인트: 「(さ)せていただけませんか」=정중한 허가 요청. 비즈니스 정형.

questions.stem:
Ａ「ねえ、『星の物語』って読んだことある？」
Ｂ「ううん、３巻（　　　）読んだよ。最新の４巻はまだ読んでないんだけど。」
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 3: までなら is correct. It means “(up) to … (at most).”

Question Translation
“A: Hey, have you ever read ‘Star Stories’?
B: No, I’ve read up to volume 3. I haven’t read the newest volume 4 yet.”

Explanation
Ｎ＋までなら = “if it’s up to N (and not beyond).” ３巻**までなら**読んだ.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. ぐらいでも — “even about/around” → Vague and unnatural here.
2. ずつなら — “if … each” → Not about quantity per action; incorrect pattern.
4. からでも — “even from … (as a starting point)” → Doesn’t express ‘up to’.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
まで (limit endpoint) vs. から (starting point); ～までなら often implies “but not after that.”
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 「までなら」 — ~까지라면.
오답: 1 「ぐらいでも」(정도라도), 2 「ずつなら」(씩이라면), 4 「からでも」(부터라도)는 '상한'의 뜻이 없음.
포인트: 「Nまでなら」=~까지의 범위 안이라면. 한계 설정.

questions.stem: 駅前にできた喫茶店はコーヒーが20種類以上あるそうだ。友達に（　　　）と誘われたので、今度一緒に行くことにした。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 1: 行ってみないか is correct. It means “Wanna go try (it)?” (invitation).

Question Translation
“I heard the café that opened in front of the station has over 20 kinds of coffee. A friend invited me, saying ‘Why don’t we go try it?’, so I decided to go together next time.”

Explanation
～ないか (volitional invitation) fits 誘われた (was invited) as reported speech.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
2. 行ったらいい — “you should go” → Advice, not invitation.
3. 行っただろう — “(we) would have gone” → Hypothetical past; unnatural.
4. 行ってみて — “try going (and then …)” → Request/instruction form, not used in an invitation quote.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
Informal invitation patterns: ～（し）ない？／～（し）てみないか. Reported with ～と誘われた／言われた.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 「行ってみないか」 — 가 보지 않을래?(권유).
오답: 2 「行ったらいい」(가는 게 좋아 — 조언), 3 「行っただろう」(갔겠지 — 추측), 4 「行ってみて」(가 봐 — 명령적 권유)는 「誘(さそ)われた」(권유받았다)의 인용 내용으로 부자연.
포인트: 「～ないか」(권유의문)이 친구 간 권유 정형.

questions.stem: 今日の夕方、聞きたいことがあって営業部の南さんのところに行ったが、もう（　　　）いなかった。
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 4: 帰ってしまっていて is correct. It means “had (already) gone home,” emphasizing completion before the speaker arrived.

Question Translation
“This evening I went to the Sales Department to see Mr. Minami to ask something, but he had already gone home.”

Explanation
～てしまっている shows a resultant state from a completed action at a reference time.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 帰っていく前に — “before (he) goes home” → Contradicts the outcome.
2. 帰っている間は — “while (he) is returning” → Odd meaning.
3. 帰ってきたまま — “as (he) came back” → Nonsense here.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
過去のある時点での結果状態: ～てしまっていた／～てしまっていて. Often translates as “had already …”.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 「帰(かえ)ってしまっていて」 — 이미 가 버려서. 결과 상태+이유.
오답: 1 「帰っていく前に」(가기 전에)는 시점이 어긋남, 2 「帰っている間は」(가는 중에는)는 의미 불명, 3 「帰ってきたまま」(돌아온 채로)는 문맥 불일치.
포인트: 「～てしまっている」=완료된 결과 지속. て형 연결로 이유까지.

questions.stem:
（公園で）
山下「この木は本当に大きいですね。」
林「そうですね。ここまで（　　　）20年ぐらいかかったそうですよ。」
opts: 大きいほうがよければ
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 1: 大きくなるのに is correct. It means “for (it) to grow big; it took (time) to become big.”

Question Translation
“(At the park)
Yamashita: This tree is really big, isn’t it?
Hayashi: Yes. I heard it took about 20 years to grow this big.”

Explanation
V-るのに (time/money) かかる expresses the resources required to achieve a state.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
2. 大きいようだし — “it seems big, and …” → Doesn’t connect to かかった.
3. 大きくできて — “was able to make (it) big” → Implies artificial making; wrong.
4. 大きいほうがよければ — “if bigger is better” → Conditional unrelated to かかった.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
～のに時間がかかる = “it takes time to … (achieve state).”
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 「大きくなるのに」 — 크게 자라는 데에.
오답: 2 「大きいようだし」(큰 듯하고), 3 「大きくできて」(크게 만들어져), 4 「大きいほうがよければ」(큰 게 좋다면)는 「時間がかかる」와 호응 X.
포인트: 「V-るのに(時間·お金)がかかる」=~하는 데에 시간·돈이 들다.

questions.stem:
医者「この薬を、３日間、１日３回飲んでください。」
患者「わかりました。痛みがないときも、（　　　）。」
医者「はい。必ず飲んでください。」
opts: 飲まないほうがよさそうですね
飲まないといけないでしょうか
飲まなくてもしかたないですね
飲まなければいいんでしょうか
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 2: 飲まないといけないでしょうか is correct. It means “Do I have to take it (even) when there’s no pain?”

Question Translation
“Doctor: Please take this medicine three times a day for three days.
Patient: Understood. Even when there’s no pain, do I have to take it?
Doctor: Yes. Be sure to take it.”

Explanation
～ないといけない expresses obligation; ～でしょうか softens the question politely.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 飲まないほうがよさそうですね — “It seems better not to take it” → Opposite of instructions.
3. 飲まなくてもしかたないですね — “It can’t be helped even if I don’t take it” → Wrong meaning.
4. 飲まなければいいんでしょうか — “Is it okay if I don’t take it?” → Asks for permission to skip; contradicts doctor.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
Obligation forms: ～なければならない／～ないといけない. Polite question: ～でしょうか.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 「飲まないといけないでしょうか」 — 먹어야 할까요?(의무 확인).
오답: 1 「飲まないほうがよさそうですね」는 의사 지시와 정반대, 3 「飲まなくてもしかたない」(안 먹어도 어쩔 수 없다)는 어색, 4 「飲まなければいい」(안 먹으면 된다)도 의사 지시와 모순.
포인트: 「～ないといけない+でしょうか」=의무 정중 확인.

questions.expl:
Answer (★ Position)
Answer 1: で

Correct Order
3 → 2 → 1★ → 4
📌 にとって → 人生 → で → いちばん大切

Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown
③ にとって — “for/to (someone)” marks the viewpoint: 私**にとって**
② 人生 — “life,” the domain being evaluated
① で — context marker “in/as” → 人生**で** (in life)
④ いちばん大切 — “the most important”

Reconstructed Sentence
**私にとって人生でいちばん大切なのは、家族の幸せだ。**
→ “What is most important in my life is my family’s happiness.”

Why This Order Works
にとって sets the personal viewpoint; 人生で marks the scope; いちばん大切 provides the predicate that links to なのは.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 (★자리=세 번째 「で」). 올바른 순서: にとって→人生→で→いちばん大切.
포인트: 「Nにとって」(~에게 있어서)+「人生で」(인생에서)+「いちばん大切」(가장 소중)=관점·범위·평가의 정형 결합.

questions.stem: この映画は昔から大好きで今まで ______ ______ ★ ______ 感動して泣いてしまう。
questions.expl:
Answer (★ Position)
Answer 4: 何度

Correct Order
2 → 3 → 4★ → 1
📌 何度も → 見たけれど → 何度 → 見ても

Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown
② 何度も — “many times” (frequency of past viewing)
③ 見たけれど — “I have watched, but …” introduces contrast
④ 何度 — “no matter how many times (… then)” when paired with 見ても
① 見ても — “even if I watch” / “no matter how many times I watch”

Reconstructed Sentence
**この映画は昔から大好きで今まで何度も見たけれど、何度見ても感動して泣いてしまう。**
→ “I’ve loved this movie for ages and have watched it many times, but no matter how many times I watch it, I end up moved to tears.”

Why This Order Works
The first clause states repeated past experience (何度も見たけれど). The second uses 何度＋Vても to express “no matter how many times …”.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 (★자리=세 번째 「何度」). 올바른 순서: 何度も→見たけれど→何度→見ても.
포인트: 「何度も～けれど、何度～ても」=몇 번이나 ~했지만, 몇 번 ~해도. 반복+조건의 호응.

questions.stem: 新しい家は、駅に近くて便利なのだが、窓が ______ ______ ★ ______ 聞こえてきて、うるさいと感じることがある。
questions.expl:
Answer (★ Position)
Answer 3: せいで

Correct Order
2 → 1 → 3★ → 4
📌 大通り側に → ある → せいで → 車の音が

Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown
② 大通り側に — “on the main-road side” (location)
① ある — “to be/face” → 窓が大通り側に**ある**
③ せいで — “because of / due to (a negative cause)”
④ 車の音が — “the sound of cars” (subject of 聞こえてくる)

Reconstructed Sentence
**新しい家は駅に近くて便利なのだが、窓が大通り側にあるせいで、車の音が聞こえてきて、うるさいと感じることがある。**
→ “The new house is convenient because it’s near the station, but since the windows face the main road, the sound of cars can be heard and sometimes it feels noisy.”

Why This Order Works
“Location＋ある” states the window’s placement; せいで marks the negative cause leading to 車の音が聞こえる.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 (★자리=세 번째 「せいで」). 올바른 순서: 大通り側に→ある→せいで→車の音が.
포인트: 「Nに+ある+せいで」=~에 있는 탓에. 부정적 원인 「せいで」.

questions.stem: 林さんの奥さんは料理がとても上手で、ハンバーグが特においしいらしい。林さんは奥さんの ______ ______ ★ ______ ものはないとよく言っている。
questions.expl:
Answer (★ Position)
Answer 2: ほど

Correct Order
1 → 3 → 2★ → 4
📌 作る → ハンバーグ → ほど → おいしい

Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown
① 作る — “(she) makes” modifying ハンバーグ
③ ハンバーグ — “hamburg steak” (object of comparison)
② ほど — “as … as; to the extent that” (comparison marker)
④ おいしい — predicate adjective

Reconstructed Sentence
**林さんは奥さんの作るハンバーグほどおいしいものはない** とよく言っている。
→ “Mr. Hayashi often says there’s nothing as delicious as his wife’s hamburg steaks.”

Why This Order Works
Noun-modifier 作る precedes ハンバーグ; ほど forms the “as ~ as” comparison used with ものはない to express a superlative.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 (★자리=세 번째 「ほど」). 올바른 순서: 作る→ハンバーグ→ほど→おいしい.
포인트: 「N(연체수식)+ほど+형용사+ものはない」=~만큼 ~한 것은 없다(최상급 비교).

opts: 妻との初めてのデートで
questions.expl:
Answer (★ Position)
Answer 2: 妻との初めてのデートで

Correct Order
4 → 1 → 2★ → 3
📌 このレストランで → 食事をするたび → 妻との初めてのデートで → 来たとき

Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown
④ このレストランで — “at this restaurant” (setting)
① 食事をするたび — “every time I eat” (frequency trigger)
② 妻との初めてのデートで — “on my first date with my wife” (modifier)
③ 来たとき — “when (we) came” completing the noun clause

Reconstructed Sentence
**このレストランで食事をするたび、妻との初めてのデートで来たときのことを思い出す。**
→ “Every time I eat at this restaurant, I remember when we came here on our first date.”

Why This Order Works
“デートで来たとき” is a single noun clause; 食事をするたび sets the timing of remembering.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 (★자리=세 번째 「妻との初めてのデートで」). 올바른 순서: このレストランで→食事をするたび→妻との初めてのデートで→来たとき.
포인트: 「Vたび」(~할 때마다)+「～で来たとき」(~로 왔을 때)=장소·계기 연결. 회상의 정형.

questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 2: しか is correct. It means “only; nothing but” (with a negative verb).

Question Translation
“…But many ramen shops in Japan often have nothing but chopsticks, so eating was difficult.”

Explanation
箸**しか**ない = “there are only chopsticks (and no forks).” Matches the context of difficulty using a fork.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. だけ — “only” (affirmative) → 箸だけない would mean “only chopsticks are not there,” wrong.
3. でも — “even” → 箸でもない “not even chopsticks” doesn’t fit.
4. まで — “even to the extent of” → 箸までない sounds odd here.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
N + しか + ない = exclusive “only N (and nothing else).” Requires a negative predicate.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 「しか」 — ~밖에(부정과 호응).
오답: 1 「だけ」(만), 3 「でも」(라도), 4 「まで」(까지)는 「～ない」와의 강한 한정 뉘앙스가 없음.
포인트: 「Nしか+ない」=~밖에 없다. 라멘집에 젓가락밖에 없어 곤란했음을 강조.

questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 4: そこで is correct. It means “so/thereupon (as a response).”

Question Translation
“(The clerk said, ‘Please feel free to hold any of them.’) So, I actually picked up a few.”

Explanation
そこで indicates a step taken in response to the previous statement/situation; fits perfectly after being invited to try.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 例えば — “for example” → Not giving examples.
2. 一方で — “on the other hand” → Not contrasting two sides.
3. ところが — “however” (unexpected contrast) → No contradiction here.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
そこで often introduces a deliberate action/solution following a problem or suggestion.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 「そこで」 — 그래서(앞 상황의 결과로 행동).
오답: 1 「例えば」(예를 들어), 2 「一方で」(한편으로 — 대비), 3 「ところが」(그런데 — 역접)는 권유→행동의 흐름에 맞지 않음.
포인트: 점원의 권유→화자가 실제로 들어 봄=「そこで+행동」.

opts: 使うようになっています
使ってほしかったです
questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 1: 使うようにしました is correct. It means “I made it a habit to use (them).”

Question Translation
“From then on, I used those chopsticks at home every day.”

Explanation
V-るようにする expresses forming a habit or deliberate practice over time, matching the narrator’s daily effort.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
2. 使ってもいいです — “it’s okay to use” → Not the writer’s action.
3. 使うようになっています — “it has come to be used (system/setting)” → Passive-like description; unnatural for personal routine.
4. 使ってほしかったです — “(I) wanted (someone) to use them” → Wrong subject/meaning.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
ようにする = intentional habit-building; ようになる = change of state (often beyond one’s control).
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 「使うようにしました」 — 쓰기로 했다(습관화).
오답: 2 「使ってもいいです」(써도 됩니다 — 허가), 3 「使うようになっています」(~하게 되어 있다 — 시스템·자연 변화), 4 「使ってほしかったです」(써 줬으면 했다)는 화자 자신의 노력과 맞지 않음.
포인트: 「V-るようにする」=의식적으로 ~하도록 하다(노력·습관).

questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 4: ラーメン屋の is correct. It means “(the) ramen shop’s …”.

Question Translation
“Then, I found I could use the chopsticks at the ramen shop easily as well.”

Explanation
Context: he practiced at home; later at a ramen shop he could use **the shop’s chopsticks** easily: **ラーメン屋の**箸.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. この — “this” → Which one? Ambiguous.
2. こういう — “this kind of” → Vague; not needed.
3. 箸の専門店の — “of the chopstick specialty store” → Not those; he’s now at a ramen shop.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
N の N marks a possessive/association: 店の箸／学校の先生 etc.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 「ラーメン屋の」 — 라멘집의(젓가락).
오답: 1 「この」, 2 「こういう」는 지시 대상이 불명확, 3 「箸の専門店の」는 집에서 산 젓가락이라 모순.
포인트: 집에서 연습→라멘집에서도(가게의 젓가락이) 편하게 쓸 수 있게 되었음.

questions.expl:
Correct Answer
Answer 3: 箸のおかげです is correct. It means “It was thanks to the chopsticks.”

Question Translation
“I had long given up on using chopsticks well. The reason I came to be able to use them well was thanks to the chopsticks I bought at the specialty store.”

Explanation
N のおかげで/です expresses a positive cause/result attributed to N.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect
1. 箸のほうです — “it’s the chopsticks side” → Strange, not idiomatic.
2. 箸だったのです — “it was the chopsticks” → Too blunt; lacks causal nuance.
4. 箸だったはずです — “it must have been the chopsticks” → Uncertain; the writer states it confidently.

Grammar/Vocabulary Note
おかげ (positive cause) vs. せい (negative cause).
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 「箸のおかげです」 — 젓가락 덕분입니다.
오답: 1 「ほうです」(~쪽입니다), 2 「だったのです」(~이었습니다), 4 「だったはずです」(~이었을 겁니다)는 긍정적 원인 부여의 의미가 없음.
포인트: 「Nのおかげで/です」=N 덕분에. 긍정적 결과의 원인.

questions.stem: カレンさんが田中先生に最も聞きたいことは何か。
opts: 自分が日本にいる間で、田中先生が大学にいる日はいつか
自分が休みの間に、田中先生と京都か広島で会えるかどうか
７月１３日から１５日の間で、田中先生と会える日があるかどうか
７月に田中先生に会うためには、いつ東京に行けばいいか
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 3 is correct. It means "Whether there is a day to meet between July 13 and 15."

**Question Translation**
"What does Karen most want to ask Professor Tanaka?"

**Explanation**
In the letter, Karen says she will be in Tokyo **from the 13th to the 15th** and would like to visit the university to meet the professor. She asks for an email reply **if there is a convenient day** during that period. Therefore, the main question is whether there is a day **between July 13–15** when they can meet.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 自分が日本にいる間で、田中先生が大学にいる日はいつか — "While I am in Japan, on which days are you at the university?" ❌
→ Too broad. She narrows it to **13–15 in Tokyo**, not the whole time in Japan.

2. 自分が休みの間に、田中先生と京都か広島で会えるかどうか — "During my vacation, whether we can meet in Kyoto or Hiroshima." ❌
→ She will **tour Kyoto and Hiroshima**, but she proposes meeting **in Tokyo**, not in those cities.

4. ７月に田中先生に会うためには、いつ東京に行けばいいか — "When should I go to Tokyo in July to meet you?" ❌
→ She already **will be in Tokyo 13–15**; she isn’t asking **when to go**.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 — 7월 13~15일 사이에 만날 수 있는 날이 있는지.
근거: 「13日から15日の間は東京にいます…ご都合のいい日がありましたら、メールでお返事を」 — 그 사이에 가능한 날을 묻는 편지.
오답: 1은 시점이 다름, 2는 교토·히로시마 만남이 아님, 4는 카렌이 도쿄 일정을 이미 정함.

questions.stem: 「私」は、自分の家が片付いていない状態になるのは、なぜだと考えているか。
opts: 「私」も夫も子供も、みんな「片付けられない人」だから
部屋が狭すぎて、物を入れる場所が少なすぎるから
「私」が、あまり部屋の片付けをしようとしないから
夫と子供が、物を使った後で初めにあった場所に戻さないから
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 4 is correct. It means "Because the husband and child don’t return things to where they were after using them."

**Question Translation**
"Why does the speaker think her home ends up untidy?"

**Explanation**
The speaker complains that family members leave clothes and toys out and says tidying would be easy **if they just put things back where they were** after use. So the cause is the husband and child **not returning items**.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 「私」も夫も子供も、みんな「片付けられない人」だから — "Because I, my husband, and my child are all people who can’t tidy up." ❌
→ She is actually **good at tidying** and is stressed by others’ mess.

2. 部屋が狭すぎて、物を入れる場所が少なすぎるから — "Because the room is too small and there’s too little storage." ❌
→ Family **says** this, but the narrator rejects it as an excuse.

3. 「私」が、あまり部屋の片付けをしようとしないから — "Because I don’t really try to tidy up." ❌
→ Opposite; she is the one **most bothered** and tries to keep it tidy.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 남편과 아이가 물건을 사용한 뒤 원래 자리에 돌려놓지 않기 때문.
근거: 「使い終わったら、それをあった所に戻すだけでいいのに、なぜこんな簡単なことができないのだろう」 — 핵심 불만.
오답: 1 모두 '못 치우는 사람'이라곤 하지 않음(가족에 한정), 2 좁은 방은 변명, 3 화자는 치우려 함.

questions.stem: この文章を書いた人は、どのように暗証番号を決めるのがよいと言っているか。
opts: 自分に関係のある数字や単純な数字にする。
自分に関係のある数字に単純な数字を足す。
自分に関係のない数字や不規則に並んだ数字にする。
分に関係のある数字に不規則に並んだ数字を足す。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 2 is correct. It means "Use a number related to yourself plus a simple number."

**Question Translation**
"How does the writer say it’s good to decide a PIN number?"

**Explanation**
The passage proposes adding a simple sequence like **1111** to a personal number (e.g., birthdate **1025 + 1111 → 2136**) so it’s **hard for others to guess** yet **easy for you to recall**.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 自分に関係のある数字や単純な数字にする。 — "Use a personal number or a simple number." ❌
→ Using them **as-is** is risky/easy to guess.

3. 自分に関係のない数字や不規則に並んだ数字にする。 — "Use unrelated or random-looking numbers." ❌
→ Safer, but **easy to forget**, which the text warns about.

4. 分に関係のある数字に不規則に並んだ数字を足す。 — "Add random numbers to a personal number." ❌
→ Not suggested; the advice is to add a **simple** number, not a random string.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 자신과 관련된 숫자에 단순한 숫자를 더한다.
근거: 「誕生日の数字『1025』に『1111』を足した『2136』を暗証番号にする」 — 본문이 제시한 방법.
오답: 1·3은 본문이 각각 '위험'·'잊기 쉬움'으로 거부한 선택지, 4는 '불규칙'을 더하는 게 아님.

questions.stem: 野村課長がこのメールで伝えたいこととして、合っているのはどれか。
opts: オオタ工業への到着時間が、訪問する時間より３０分くらい遅くなりそうなこと
待ち合わせる所を、駅前の喫茶店からオオタ工業の入口前に変えること
時間がないので、最終確認をせずにオオタ工業を訪問することにしたこと
オオタ工業を訪問する前の最終確認ができないときは、もう一度連絡すること
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 2 is correct. It means "Change the meeting place from the café in front of the station to the entrance of Oota Kōgyō."

**Question Translation**
"Which statement correctly expresses what Section Chief Nomura wants to convey in this email?"

**Explanation**
Due to a delay, Nomura says there is no time for the café meeting. He will go **directly from the station to Oota Kōgyō** and asks Uchida to **wait at the entrance**. That is the key change.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. オオタ工業への到着時間が、訪問する時間より３０分くらい遅くなりそうなこと — "Arrival will be ~30 minutes later than the appointment time." ❌
→ 30 minutes is the **train delay estimate**, not a stated arrival time.

3. 時間がないので、最終確認をせずにオオタ工業を訪問することにしたこと — "They decided to visit without any final check." ❌
→ He suggests doing a **quick final check at the entrance** if time allows.

4. オオタ工業を訪問する前の最終確認ができないときは、もう一度連絡すること — "If they can’t do the final check, he’ll contact again." ❌
→ He says he’ll contact again **if they can’t make the visit time**, not about the final check.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 만날 장소를 역앞 찻집에서 오오타공업 입구 앞으로 바꾼다.
근거: 「私は駅から直接向かうので、先にオオタ工業に行って、入口前で待っていてください」 — 본문의 핵심 부탁.
오답: 1은 도착 시각은 본문에서 단정 X, 3은 시간이 있으면 입구 앞에서 최종 확인한다고 함, 4는 늦을 때만 다시 연락.

questions.stem: 「私」が手帳を選ぶときに最も大切だと感じているのは何か。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 4 is correct. It means "the color of the cover."

**Question Translation**
"What does the writer feel is most important when choosing a planner?"

**Explanation**
The writer explicitly says the most important thing is the **cover**, and among cover features they **choose by color**. The planner’s color influences their mood because they see it many times a day.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 書きやすさ、見やすさ — "Ease of writing / ease of reading." ❌
→ Mentioned as generally important, but **not** the writer’s top priority.

2. 大きさ — "Size." ❌
→ Also called important, but **not the most** important for this writer.

3. 表紙の絵や柄 — "Cover picture/pattern." ❌
→ Some people choose by this, but the writer says **they choose by color**.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 표지의 색.
근거: 「私がいちばん大切にしているのは、表紙だ。絵や柄で選ぶ人もいるが、私は色で選ぶ」 — 표지 중에서도 '색'을 가장 중시.
오답: 1·2는 일반론으로 언급, 3 '그림·무늬'는 다른 사람의 선택 기준.

questions.stem: 「私」にとって、手帳はどのようなものか。
opts: いつも身近にあって、自分が家族や親友の次に大切にしているもの
いつも身近にあって、自分の気持ちに影響を与えるもの
いつも身近にあって、自分にきちんとした生活をさせてくれるもの
いつも身近にあって、自分の生き方を示してくれるもの
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 2 is correct. It means "It’s always close at hand and influences my feelings."

**Question Translation**
"For the writer, what kind of thing is a planner?"

**Explanation**
They carry it everywhere, take it out many times a day, and say the **color affects their mood**. Thus it’s something always nearby that **influences their feelings**.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. いつも身近にあって、自分が家族や親友の次に大切にしているもの — "Always close; second only to family/friends in importance." ❌
→ They say they spend **more time** with it than with family, but not that it ranks **second in importance**.

3. いつも身近にあって、自分にきちんとした生活をさせてくれるもの — "Always close; makes me live neatly/regularly." ❌
→ The text focuses on **mood influence**, not enforcing orderly life.

4. いつも身近にあって、自分の生き方を示してくれるもの — "Always close; shows my way of life." ❌
→ Too grand; not stated.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 늘 곁에 있고, 자신의 기분에 영향을 주는 것.
근거: 「いつも手帳を持ち歩く…手帳の色に影響を受けていると感じる」 — 색이 기분에 영향.
오답: 1 가족·친한 친구 '다음'이 아님, 3 단정한 생활·4 삶의 방식은 본문에 없음.

questions.stem: 今年は手帳選びに時間がかかっているのはなぜか。
opts: 時間に対する考え方が少し変わってきたから
仕事に対する気持ちが少し変わってきたから
ストレスに対する姿勢が少し変わってきたから
色に対する感覚が少し変わってきたから
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 2 is correct. It means "Because the writer’s feelings toward work have changed a bit."

**Question Translation**
"Why is choosing a planner taking longer this year?"

**Explanation**
Turning **30**, the writer wants to work with a **calmer mindset**, so they’re torn between energizing bright colors and calming dark colors. This reflects a **change in attitude toward work**, not time, stress policy, or color sense itself.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 時間に対する考え方が少し変わってきたから — "Their view of time changed." ❌
→ The change is about **work mood**, not time management.

3. ストレスに対する姿勢が少し変わってきたから — "Their stance on stress changed." ❌
→ Stressful work is mentioned, but the key is **wanting calm at work**.

4. 色に対する感覚が少し変わってきたから — "Their sense of color changed." ❌
→ No; the dilemma is **which color suits the new work feeling**.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 일에 대한 마음가짐이 조금 바뀌었기 때문.
근거: 「30歳になり、もっと落ち着いた気持ちで仕事をしたいと思うようになってきた」 — 일에 임하는 자세 변화로 색 선택 고민.
오답: 1 시간관, 3 스트레스 자세, 4 색 감각 자체의 변화는 본문에 명시되지 않음.
questions.stem_u: 今年は手帳選びに時間がかかっている

questions.stem: 娘が①「お母さん、青。」と言ったのは、なぜか。
opts: 青信号なのに、「私」が車を止めたから
「私」が慌ててアクセルを踏んだから
涙が出そうになったことを「私」に知られたくなかったから
信号が変わったことに「私」が気づいていなかったから
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 4 is correct. It means "Because I hadn’t noticed the light had changed."

**Question Translation**
"Why did the daughter say, ‘Mom, it’s blue’?"

**Explanation**
The narrator stopped at a **red** light, got absorbed in a nostalgic song, and the light turned **blue/green** without her noticing. The daughter prompted her to go.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 青信号なのに、「私」が車を止めたから — "She stopped even though it was blue." ❌
→ She stopped **when it was red**; it turned blue later.

2. 「私」が慌ててアクセルを踏んだから — "Because she hastily pressed the accelerator." ❌
→ That happened **after** the daughter spoke.

3. 涙が出そうになったことを「私」に知られたくなかったから — "She didn’t want me to notice she was about to cry." ❌
→ Not supported; the **mother** was the one getting teary.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 신호가 바뀐 것을 「私」가 알아채지 못했기 때문.
근거: 빨간불에서 멈춰 노래에 빠져 있다가 「青(파란불)」으로 바뀐 걸 모르자 딸이 알려 줌 → 「慌ててアクセルを踏んだ」.
오답: 1·2는 인과가 반대, 3 눈물은 딸의 발화 이유와 무관.

questions.stem: 「私」にとって、②この曲はどんな曲か。
opts: 苦しい時に自分を慰めてくれた思い出の曲
苦しい時代の自分を思い出させる嫌いな曲
若いころの自分を思い出したい時に聞く大切な曲
若いころの悩んでいた自分に聞かせたい曲
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 1 is correct. It means "A memorable song that comforted me when times were hard."

**Question Translation**
"For the narrator, what kind of song is ‘this’ song?"

**Explanation**
In her early 20s she often failed at work and cried; the words "Don’t lose. You’re not alone." **comforted** her and made her feel like trying again. So it’s a supportive, comforting song from tough times.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
2. 苦しい時代の自分を思い出させる嫌いな曲 — "A hated song that reminds her of hard times." ❌
→ She **likes** it; it was her **cheering song**.

3. 若いころの自分を思い出したい時に聞く大切な曲 — "A precious song she listens to when she wants to recall youth." ❌
→ She doesn’t **choose** to listen for nostalgia; it’s meaningful because it **supported** her.

4. 若いころの悩んでいた自分に聞かせたい曲 — "A song she wants to play for her troubled younger self." ❌
→ Not stated; focus is on how it **helped her then**.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 — 힘들 때 나를 위로해 준 추억의 곡.
근거: 「『負けないで。君は一人じゃない。』という言葉に慰められ、頑張ろうという気持ちになりました」 — 위로·격려.
오답: 2 '싫은 곡'이 아니라 소중한 곡, 3·4는 본문 진술과 다름.

questions.stem: ③今はきっと幸せなのだとあるが、なぜそう思うのか。
opts: 今は家族を応援してあげられるようになったから
今は「応援歌」を聞く必要のない毎日が過ごせているから
今でも苦しいことがあるが、「応援歌」を聞くと頑張れるから
今でも泣きたくなるようなことはあるが、応援してくれる家族がいるから
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 2 is correct. It means "Because now she spends her days without needing to listen to a ‘cheering song.’"

**Question Translation**
"She says, ‘I must be happy now.’ Why does she think so?"

**Explanation**
She says the painful things that made her want to cry have **disappeared** over the years, and she heard this song **for the first time in a long while** today. That implies she **no longer needs** it daily, hence she feels happy now.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 今は家族を応援してあげられるようになったから — "Because she can now support her family." ❌
→ Not mentioned.

3. 今でも苦しいことがあるが、「応援歌」を聞くと頑張れるから — "She still has hardships but can do her best by listening to it." ❌
→ The text says such hardships have **vanished**.

4. 今でも泣きたくなるようなことはあるが、応援してくれる家族がいるから — "She still wants to cry sometimes but has supportive family." ❌
→ Not stated; emphasis is on **not needing** the song anymore.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 지금은 '응원가'를 들을 필요 없는 매일을 보내고 있어서.

오답: 1 가족 응원, 3·4 지금도 힘들다는 진술과는 모순.
questions.stem_u: ③今はきっと幸せなのだ

questions.stem: 川村さんは、どうして「みんなの庭」に参加したのか。
opts: 広い場所で野菜や花を育てたかったから
マンションで野菜を作ってはいけないから
「みんなの庭」を借りるのが夢だったから
近所の人と友達になりたかったから
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 1 is correct. It means "Because she wanted to grow vegetables and flowers in a large space."

**Question Translation**
"Why did Ms. Kawamura join ‘Everyone’s Garden’?"

**Explanation**
She loves growing plants but couldn’t fully enjoy it on her apartment balcony. Her **long-time dream** was to grow them in a home with a **large yard**, so the community garden fulfilled that need for space.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
2. マンションで野菜を作ってはいけないから — "Because making vegetables at the condo is forbidden." ❌
→ Not stated; the issue is **insufficient space/enjoyment**.

3. 「みんなの庭」を借りるのが夢だったから — "Because renting ‘Everyone’s Garden’ was her dream." ❌
→ Her dream was a **large private yard**, not specifically this program.

4. 近所の人と友達になりたかったから — "Because she wanted to be friends with neighbors." ❌
→ Friendships are a **result**, not the original reason.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 — 넓은 곳에서 채소와 꽃을 키우고 싶어서.
근거: 「マンションのベランダでは十分に楽しむことができない。広い庭のある家で、野菜や花を育てることが、長い間の夢」.
오답: 2 맨션에서 금지란 언급 없음, 3 '빌리는 것이 꿈'은 아님, 4 친구는 결과적 부수 효과.

questions.stem: 「みんなの庭」はどのようなものか。
opts: 市から借りた土地で、市の意見を聞いてみんなで野菜や花を育てる庭
市から借りた土地で、一人一人が好きなように野菜や花を育てていく庭
市から借りた土地で、使う人が話し合って一緒に野菜や花を育てていく庭
市から借りた土地で、市が立てた計画に従って一人ずつ野菜や花を育てる庭
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 3 is correct. It means "A garden on city-rented land where users discuss and grow vegetables and flowers together."

**Question Translation**
"What kind of thing is ‘Everyone’s Garden’?"

**Explanation**
The passage defines it as **city-owned land rented to citizens** where **users talk things over and collectively grow** vegetables and flowers.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 市の意見を聞いて… — "On city-rented land where they listen to the city’s opinions and grow together." ❌
→ Decisions are by **the users**, not by city instructions.

2. 一人一人が好きなように… — "On city-rented land where each person grows as they like individually." ❌
→ It’s **collaborative**, not individual plots acting independently.

4. 市の計画に従って一人ずつ… — "According to a city plan, each person grows individually." ❌
→ No city plan; it’s **user discussion and cooperation**.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 — 시(市)에서 빌린 땅에서, 이용자가 의논해 함께 채소·꽃을 키우는 정원.
근거: 「市の土地を市民が借りて、共同の『庭』を作っていく…利用する人たちが話し合って、みんなで野菜や花を育てて楽しむ」.
오답: 1 '시의 의견'은 본문에 없음, 2·4 '한 사람씩'·'시의 계획'은 본문과 반대.

questions.stem: ①「みんなの庭」では違うとあるが、どんな点が違うのか。
opts: 野菜や花がたくさん作れる点
花の名前を教えてもらえる点
近所の人とあいさつができる点
いろいろな人と友達になれる点
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 4 is correct. It means "The point that she can make friends with various people."

**Question Translation**
"‘It’s different in Everyone’s Garden’—in what way is it different?"

**Explanation**
Unlike the usual neighborhood where it was hard to become friends, in the garden she naturally **chats, has tea, and makes many friends**. Thus, the key difference is **forming friendships with various people**.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. 野菜や花がたくさん作れる点 — "You can grow many plants." ❌
→ Not the contrasted point here.

2. 花の名前を教えてもらえる点 — "You can be taught flower names." ❌
→ Not mentioned.

3. 近所の人とあいさつができる点 — "You can greet neighbors." ❌
→ She could already **greet** them; the change is **becoming friends**.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 다양한 사람과 친구가 될 수 있는 점.
근거: 「『きれいに咲きましたね』とか…話しかけたり…お茶を飲んだり、おしゃべりをしたり…自然にできる」 — 자연스러운 교류로 친구가 늘었음.
오답: 1 채소 양·2 꽃 이름·3 인사는 평소에도 가능 — 핵심 차이가 아님.

questions.stem: ②そう感じているとあるが、どのように感じているのか。
opts: もっとたくさんの野菜や花が作れるようになるかもしれない。
近所の人とももっと交流することができるようになるかもしれない。
町をもっとよくするための話し合いもできるようになるかもしれない。
「みんなの庭」をもっとよくすることができるようになるかもしれない。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 3 is correct. It means "They may be able to hold discussions to make the town better."

**Question Translation**
"‘She feels so’—what does she feel?"

**Explanation**
As relationships deepen, they’ve started talking about education and safety. She feels that continuing these ties may lead to **discussions about making the town more livable**.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
1. もっとたくさんの野菜や花が作れる — "They might be able to grow more plants." ❌
→ Not the point of that sentence.

2. 近所の人とももっと交流できる — "They might interact more with neighbors." ❌
→ Interaction is already happening; the new possibility is **civic discussion**.

4. 「みんなの庭」をもっとよくできる — "They might improve the garden itself." ❌
→ The focus is **improving the town**, not the garden.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 — 마을을 더 좋게 만들기 위한 의논도 할 수 있게 될지 모른다.

questions.stem: ロアンさんは、はらクリーニングの会員で、４月３日（火）にセーターとコートを１枚ずつクリーニングに出したいと思っている。ロアンさんは、いくら支払わなければならないか。
opts: セーター240円とコート780円
セーター400円とコート1300円
セーター240円とコート780円と会費300円
セーター400円とコート1300円と会費300円
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 1: セーター240円とコート780円 is correct.

**Question Translation**  
Roan is a member of Hara Cleaning and wants to have one sweater and one coat cleaned on Tuesday, April 3rd. How much does he have to pay?

**Explanation**  
According to the advertisement, the special sale runs from **March 24 (Sat) to April 10 (Tue)**. During this period, **members receive 40% off the basic cleaning fee**. Since Roan is already a member and plans to bring his clothes on **April 3rd (within the sale period)**, he gets the 40% discount.

From the price list:
- Sweater (セーター): 400円 → 240円 after 40% discount  
- Coat (コート): 1300円 → 780円 after 40% discount  

Therefore, total payment = **240円 + 780円 = 1020円**.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
2️⃣ セーター400円とコート1300円  
→ “Sweater 400 yen and coat 1300 yen.” ❌ These are the basic prices without the 40% discount, but Roan is a member during the sale period.

3️⃣ セーター240円とコート780円と会費300円  
→ “Sweater 240 yen, coat 780 yen, and membership fee 300 yen.” ❌ The membership fee only applies if you are **not yet a member**, but Roan **already is** one.

4️⃣ セーター400円とコート1300円と会費300円  
→ “Sweater 400 yen, coat 1300 yen, and membership fee 300 yen.” ❌ Incorrect for both price and membership reason.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- 「～中（ちゅう）」 means “during” — used in “セール期間中,” meaning “during the sale period.”  
- 「割引（わりびき）」 means “discount.” Here, 「40％割引」 = “40% off.”
questions.expl_ko:

근거: 4월 3일은 세일 기간(3/24~4/10) 안. 회원은 기본 요금에서 40% 할인 → 스웨터 400→240엔, 코트 1300→780엔. 회비는 이미 회원이므로 불필요.
오답: 2 할인 전 금액, 3·4는 회비 300엔을 추가해 잘못 계산.

questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**
Answer 1 is correct. It means "Pickup cannot be on the same day; it’s from the day after the phone application."

**Question Translation**
"For someone who wants to use Hara Cleaning’s home-visit service, which point must they be careful about?"

**Explanation**
Ad B says: apply **by phone during business hours**; **翌日以降** (from the **next day onward**), they will come within the time window you specified to pick up your clothes. Therefore, same-day pickup is **not available**.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**
2. 午前１０時から午後６時の間でなければ、取りに来てもらえないこと — "They only pick up between 10:00 and 18:00." ❌
→ Visit hours for pickups are **18:00–22:00** on business days.

3. 土曜日と日曜日は、届けてもらえないこと — "They don’t deliver on Saturdays and Sundays." ❌
→ Delivery date/time must be chosen from **営業日 (business days)**; the shop is closed **Sundays/holidays**, but **Saturdays are business days**, so Saturday delivery is possible.

4. 届けてもらう日は、指定できないこと — "You can’t specify the delivery day." ❌
→ You **do specify** the delivery day/time when applying (from about **one week after pickup**, within business days).

**Vocabulary Note**
翌日以降（よくじついこう）= from the next day onward; 営業日（えいぎょうび）= business day.

근거: 광고 B의 방문 서비스 안내에 신청 「翌日以降」 픽업 가능 명시.
오답: 2 10~18시 운영은 영업시간 안내(다른 조건), 3 토·일 배달 불가는 본문 진술과 다름, 4 배달일 지정은 가능.

questions.translation_en:
A man and a woman are talking. Why did the woman recently start attending swimming lessons?

M: Aoki, I heard you recently started going to a swimming class.
F: Yeah. I learned the basics when I was a child, but since becoming an office worker, I’ve lost stamina. I thought swimming might help me avoid catching colds and make me less tired.
M: Oh, they say swimming makes you stronger. Is the class fun?
F: Yes, recently I’ve been able to swim longer distances more easily, so I’m glad I joined.
M: When do you go?
F: On weekday evenings. There are a lot of other office workers, and I’ve made some friends too.
M: That’s nice.

questions.translation_en:
On television, a newscaster is talking about the results of a survey. What does the announcer say was the most popular future dream for boys this year?

F: What do you want to be when you grow up? This year again, a survey was conducted with about 1,000 elementary school students. Among girls, cake shop owner became very popular, moving from 10th to 2nd place. Among boys, the number who wanted to become scientists suddenly increased since last year. This time, the most common answer among girls was “elementary school teacher,” same as last year. For boys, it was “police officer.” Ten years ago, the top answer for both boys and girls was “doctor,” but things seem to have changed recently.

What does the announcer say was the number one dream for boys this year?

questions.translation_en:
A husband and wife are talking on a mobile phone. Why does the husband say he’ll be home late today?

M: Hello?
F: Where are you? It’s raining heavily — aren’t the trains stopped?
M: Oh, I’m still at the office. I’m not sure about the trains, but I’ll probably be late today because there’s a meeting after this.
F: I see. That’s tough. Are you eating dinner with your coworkers?

女おんなの人ひとは小学校しょうがっこうを卒業そつぎょうしたとき、箱はこに何を入いれたと言いっていますか。
questions.translation_en:
On the radio, a woman is talking. What does she say she put in the box when she graduated from elementary school?

F: At the elementary school I graduated from, there was something called a “graduation memory box.” Each student put one memorable item into the box, and it was buried in the schoolyard for 20 years. After 20 years, we opened it. Some people had put in perfect-score tests, photos with friends, or CDs of their favorite singers. Some even put letters to their future selves. I was one of those, and my letter said, “Are you a singer now?” At that time, I wanted to become a singer. In the end, I didn’t, but now I enjoy working as a songwriter.

What does the woman say she put in the box when she graduated from elementary school?

次の国際交流会こくさいこうりゅうかいで、何をすることにしましたか。
questions.translation_en:
At the city hall, a man and a woman are talking. What did they decide to do at the next international exchange event?

M: We need to decide soon what to do for the next international exchange event between foreign students and local residents. Do you have any good ideas? I was thinking a sports tournament or a cooking class might be interesting.
F: Hmm, for an exchange event, how about something like a conversation class where Japanese people teach Japanese to the foreign students, and the students teach their languages to the Japanese?
M: Ah, we did something similar just last year.
F: Right. We also had a karaoke contest where people sang Japanese songs before, didn’t we?
M: Yeah, it was fun, but the students and locals didn’t have much time to talk, so there wasn’t much actual exchange.
F: Then how about a sports tournament? People can cheer, talk to each other, and it would naturally create chances to interact.
M: That’s true. It sounds like a good way to connect. Let’s do that this time.
F: A cooking class would also create lots of interaction, so maybe keep that idea for another time.
M: Yeah, thanks.

What did they decide to do at the next international exchange event?

questions.script_html:

M：うん、そう。
F：どうですか。私が最近さいきん読よんだ本ほんは文章ぶんしょうが分わかりにくくて、途中とちゅうで読よみたくなくなっちゃったんですよ。
M：それはないけど、面白おもしろそうだと思って買ったんだけど、想像そうぞうしてたのと違ちがったよ。
F：そうなんですか。
M：書かいてあることが基礎的きそてきなこととか、もう知しってることばかりで…。

questions.translation_en:
At a university, a female and a male student are talking about a book.

F: Is this book called “The Secrets of Economics” yours, senpai?
M: Yeah, that’s right.
F: How is it? The book I recently read was hard to understand, and I lost interest halfway through.
M: It wasn’t like that, but I bought it thinking it looked interesting, and it turned out different from what I expected.
F: Oh, really?
M: It’s full of basic things that I already know…
F: So, for you, it wasn’t really full of “secrets,” huh?
M: It’s written in simple language, so I think it’s a good introductory book.

What does the male student say about this book?
1. It was easy to read and matched his expectations.
2. It was easy to read, but different from his expectations.
3. It was hard to read but matched his expectations.
4. It was hard to read and different from his expectations.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (読みやすいが、期待した内容と違った). 근거: 음성 「想像そうぞうしてたのと違ちがったよ…簡単かんたんな言葉ことばで書かいてあるから、入門にゅうもんの本ほんとしてはいい」 — 읽기 쉽지만 기대와 다름. 오답: 1. 기대 내용과 같지 않음. 2. 정답. 3. 읽기 쉬움. 4. 읽기 쉬움. 포인트: 「想像そうぞうしてたのと違ちがった」=상상한 것과 달랐다 / 「簡単かんたんな言葉ことば」=쉬운 말. 평가 표현.

questions.script_html:
大学だいがくのクラブで女おんなの学生がくせいと男おとこの学生がくせいが話はなしています。

F：木村きむらくん、週末しゅうまつのクラブの旅行りょこう、行いく？
M：うん、行いくよ。
F：ねえ、木村きむらくんって、写真しゃしんを撮とるのが趣味しゅみだって言いってたよね。
M：そうだけど、どうして。
F：じつは、私、旅行りょこうでみんなの写真しゃしんを撮とる、写真しゃしんの係かかりを引ひき受うけてるんだ。でも、昨日きのう、私の[カメラ]が壊こわれちゃって、週末しゅうまつまでに直なおりそうにないんだ。それで、悪わるいんだけど、木村きむらくんに頼たのみたいと思って。
M：ああ、僕、使つかってない[カメラ]があるから、貸かそうか。
F：ありがとう。でも、木村きむらくんの[カメラ]だと、撮とり方かたとかよく分わからないし、木村きむらくんのほうが撮とるのが上手じょうずだと思うから。お願いおねがいできない。
M：そう。うん、分わかった。

女おんなの学生がくせいが言いいたいことは何ですか。
1　壊こわれた[カメラ]を直なおしてほしい。
2　[カメラ]を貸かしてほしい。
3　写真しゃしんの撮とり方かたを教おしえてほしい。
4　写真しゃしんの係かかりを変かわってほしい。
questions.translation_en:
At a university club, a female and a male student are talking.

F: Kimura, are you going on the club trip this weekend?
M: Yeah, I’m going.
F: Hey, Kimura, you said taking photos is your hobby, right?
M: That’s right. Why?
F: Actually, I’m in charge of taking everyone’s photos on the trip. But yesterday, my camera broke, and it probably won’t be fixed by the weekend. So, I feel bad, but I’d like to ask you a favor.
M: Oh, I have a camera I’m not using. Should I lend it to you?
F: Thank you. But with your camera, I wouldn’t know how to use it well, and I think you’re better at taking photos than I am. So, I can’t ask you that.
M: I see. Okay, I got it.

What does the female student want to say?
1. She wants him to fix her broken camera.
2. She wants to borrow his camera.
3. She wants him to teach her how to take photos.
4. She wants him to take over as the person in charge of photos.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (写真の係を変わってほしい). 근거: 음성 「私の[カメラ]が壊こわれちゃって…木村きむらくんのほうが撮とるのが上手じょうずだと思うから。お願いおねがいできない」 — 사진 담당을 바꿔 달라는 부탁. 오답: 1. 카메라 수리는 「直なおりそうにない」 불가능. 2. 카메라 빌려준다는 제안 거절. 3. 촬영법은 이유의 일부. 4. 정답. 포인트: 「~を変かわってほしい」=~을 바꿔 줬으면 좋겠다. 역할 교체 의뢰.

questions.script_html:
テレビで女おんなの人ひとが話はなしています。

F：秋あきの山やま、赤あかや黄色きいろの木きの葉はがきれいですね。そんな景色けしきを楽たのしめる旅行りょこうはいかがですか。ここ、山川やまかわ旅館りょかんの近ちかくには人気にんきのサイクリングコースがあり、この旅館りょかんでは、自転車じてんしゃに乗のったままチェックイン、自転車じてんしゃも部屋へやの中なかに置おけるんです。旅館りょかんの方かたにサイクリングコースについて相談そうだんすることもできます。山やまを走はしったあと、温泉おんせんで汗あせを流ながすのもまた気持きもちがよさそうですね。

女おんなの人ひとは何について話はなしていますか。
1　秋あきに人気にんきがあるスポーツ
2　自転車じてんしゃ旅行りょこうに便利べんりな旅館りょかん
3　サイクリングコースの選えらび方かた
4　美うつくしい景色けしきが楽たのしめる温泉おんせん
questions.translation_en:
A woman is talking on TV.

F: The mountains in autumn, with their red and yellow leaves, are beautiful, aren’t they? How about taking a trip where you can enjoy such scenery? Near the Yamakawa Inn, there’s a popular cycling course. At this inn, you can check in while still on your bicycle, and even keep your bike in your room. You can also consult with the staff about cycling routes. After riding through the mountains, it must feel great to relax in the hot spring.

What is the woman talking about?
1. A sport popular in autumn.
2. An inn convenient for bicycle trips.
3. How to choose a cycling course.
4. A hot spring with beautiful scenery.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (自転車旅行に便利な旅館). 근거: 음성 「自転車じてんしゃに乗のったままチェックイン、自転車じてんしゃも部屋へやの中なかに置おけるんです」 — 자전거 여행에 편리한 료칸 소개. 오답: 1. 가을 인기 스포츠 자체 아님. 2. 정답. 3. 코스 선택법 아님(상담 가능 정도). 4. 온천 자체 아님(부수적). 포인트: 「~たまま」=~한 채로 / 「~に置おける」=~에 둘 수 있다. 시설 안내.

subsections.title: 問題4
subsections.intro_html: <ruby>問題<rt>もんだい</rt></ruby> 4では、えを<ruby>見<rt>み</rt></ruby>ながら<ruby>質問<rt>しつもん</rt></ruby>を聞いてください。やじるし（➡）の人は何と言いますか。1 から 3 の<ruby>中<rt>なか</rt></ruby>から、<ruby>最<rt>もっと</rt></ruby>もよいものを<ruby>一<rt>ひと</rt></ruby>つえらんでください。

questions.script_html:
レストランで客きゃくに注文ちゅうもんを聞ききます。客きゃくに何と言いいますか。
1　注文ちゅうもんを聞きいてもらえますか。
2　ご注文ちゅうもんはお決きまりになりましたか。
3　注文ちゅうもんしてもよろしいですか。
questions.translation_en:
At a restaurant, you are taking a customer’s order. What do you say to the customer?
1. May I have your order?
2. Have you decided on your order?
3. May I place the order now?
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (ご注文はお決まりになりましたか). 근거: 음성 「ご注文ちゅうもんはお決きまりになりましたか」 — 손님께 주문 결정 여부 확인. 오답: 1. 「聞きいてもらえますか」=들어 주시겠습니까(어색). 2. 정답. 3. 「注文ちゅうもんしてもよろしいですか」=주문해도 될까요(손님 측 말). 포인트: 「お+동사 연용형+になる」=존경 표현. 「お決きまりになる」=결정하시다.

questions.script_html:
公園こうえんで鳥とりに食たべ物ものをやってはいけません。友達ともだちたちが食たべ物ものをやっているので、注意ちゅういします。何と言いいますか。
1　食たべ物もの、やっちゃだめだよ。
2　食たべ物もの、気きをつけてやってね。
3　食たべ物もの、やるなって書かいてないよ。
questions.translation_en:
In the park, you are not allowed to feed the birds. Your friends are feeding them, so you warn them. What do you say?
1. Don’t feed them.
2. Be careful when feeding them.
3. It doesn’t say you can’t feed them.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (食べ物、やっちゃだめだよ). 근거: 음성 「食たべ物もの、やっちゃだめだよ」 — 금지 주의. 오답: 1. 정답. 2. 「気きをつけてやってね」=조심해서 줘(허락). 3. 「やるなって書かいてないよ」=주지 말라고 안 쓰여 있어(반대). 포인트: 「~ちゃだめ」=~하면 안 돼. 「~てはいけない」 구어형.

questions.script_html:
客きゃくに工場こうじょうを案内あんないします。何と言いいますか。
1　工場こうじょうの中なかを案内あんないしていただきます。
2　工場こうじょうの中なかをご案内あんないします。
3　工場こうじょうの中なかの案内あんないをお願ねがいします。
questions.translation_en:
You are guiding a guest around a factory. What do you say?
1. I will be guided around the factory.
2. I will guide you around the factory.
3. Please guide me around the factory.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (工場の中をご案内します). 근거: 음성 「工場こうじょうの中なかをご案内あんないします」 — 자신이 안내하는 겸양. 오답: 1. 「案内あんないしていただきます」=안내받습니다(상대가 안내). 2. 정답. 3. 「お願ねがいします」=부탁드립니다(의뢰). 포인트: 「ご+한자어+する」=겸양 표현. 「ご案内あんないする」=안내해 드리다.

questions.script_html:
友達ともだちみんなの写真しゃしんを撮とります。みんなに[カメラ]のほうを見みてほしいです。何と言いいますか。
1　ねえ、写真しゃしんを撮とって。
2　僕、写うつってる。
3　みんな、こっち向むいて。
questions.translation_en:
You are taking a picture of your friends. You want everyone to look at the camera. What do you say?
1. Hey, take a picture.
2. I’m in the photo.
3. Everyone, look this way.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (みんな、こっち向いて). 근거: 음성 「みんな、こっち向むいて」 — 카메라 쪽 보기 요청. 오답: 1. 「写真しゃしんを撮とって」=찍어 줘(요청). 2. 「僕、写うつってる」=나 찍히고 있어(확인). 3. 정답. 포인트: 「こっち向むいて」=이쪽 봐. 시선 유도.

subsections.title: 問題5
subsections.intro_html: <ruby>問題<rt>もんだい</rt></ruby> 5では、<ruby>問題用紙<rt>もんだいようし</rt></ruby>に何もいんさつされていません。まず<ruby>文<rt>ぶん</rt></ruby>を聞いてください。それから、そのへんじを聞いて、1 から 3 の<ruby>中<rt>なか</rt></ruby>から、<ruby>最<rt>もっと</rt></ruby>もよいものを<ruby>一<rt>ひと</rt></ruby>つえらんでください。

questions.script_html:
男：あの、誘さそってくださった金曜きんようのお花見おはなみですけど、行いけなくなっちゃいまして。
1　そうですか。じゃ、また今度こんど誘さそいますね。
2　えっ？お花見おはなみ、やらないことになったんですか。
3　金曜きんよう、行いけるんですね。
questions.translation_en:
M: About the cherry blossom viewing on Friday that you invited me to — I’m afraid I can’t go anymore.
1. I see. Well then, I’ll invite you again next time.
2. Huh? The flower viewing was canceled?
3. So, you can go on Friday?
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (そうですか。じゃ、また今度誘いますね). 근거: 음성 「行いけなくなっちゃいまして」에 대한 자연스러운 응답 — 다음에 다시. 오답: 1. 정답. 2. 「やらないことになった」=취소된 게 아님(혼자 못 감). 3. 「行いけるんですね」=갈 수 있군요(반대 이해). 포인트: 「~なっちゃいまして」=~하게 되어 버려서. 곤란 표현.

questions.script_html:
男：岡田おかださん、出張しゅっちょうの準備じゅんび、何か僕にできることある。
1　ありがとう。でも、大丈夫だいじょうぶ。
2　えっ？何も手伝てつだってくれないの。
3　何でも頼たのんで。
questions.translation_en:
M: Okada, is there anything I can do to help you prepare for your business trip?
1. Thank you, but I’m fine.
2. Huh? You’re not helping me at all?
3. Ask me for anything.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (ありがとう。でも、大丈夫). 근거: 음성 「何か僕にできることある」 도움 제안에 대한 응답 — 거절. 오답: 1. 정답. 2. 「何も手伝てつだってくれないの」=상대 비난(부적절). 3. 「何でも頼たのんで」=상대 입장 발화. 포인트: 「ありがとう。でも、大丈夫だいじょうぶ」=정중한 사양. 거절 표현.

questions.script_html:
女：中村なかむらさん、課長かちょうがみんなで一緒いっしょに食事しょくじでもどうかって。
1　食事しょくじおいしかったです。
2　課長かちょうが行いけないなら、また今度こんど。
3　いいですね。私も行いきます。
questions.translation_en:
F: Nakamura, the section chief suggested we all go out for a meal together.
1. The food was delicious.
2. If the section chief can’t go, let’s do it another time.
3. Sounds good. I’ll go too.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (いいですね。私も行きます). 근거: 음성 「課長かちょうがみんなで一緒いっしょに食事しょくじでもどうかって」 — 식사 권유 수락. 오답: 1. 「おいしかったです」=이미 먹은 후. 2. 「課長かちょうが行いけないなら」=과장이 못 간다는 오해. 3. 정답. 포인트: 「~でもどうかって」=~라도 어떠냐고. 권유 전달.

questions.script_html:
男：森もりさん、今朝けさのテレビのニュース、ご覧らんになりましたか。
1　ニュースは見みないですか。
2　えっ？何のニュースですか。
3　またお目めにかかってません。
questions.translation_en:
M: Mori, did you see the TV news this morning?
1. Don’t you watch the news?
2. Huh? What news was it?
3. I haven’t seen you again yet.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (えっ？何のニュースですか). 근거: 음성 「今朝けさのテレビのニュース、ご覧らんになりましたか」 — 어떤 뉴스인지 되묻기. 오답: 1. 「ニュースは見みないですか」=상대에게 질문(부적절). 2. 정답. 3. 「お目めにかかってません」=만나지 못함(엉뚱). 포인트: 「ご覧らんになる」=見みる의 존경어. 보시다.

questions.script_html:
女：あれ、小林こばやしさんが会議かいぎに遅おくれてくるはずないんだけどね。
1　何かあったのかもしれないね。
2　いつも遅おくれてくるんだ。
3　もう来きてるの。いつも早はやいね。
questions.translation_en:
F: That’s strange. Kobayashi usually isn’t late for meetings.
1. Maybe something happened.
2. He’s always late.
3. He’s already here. He’s always early.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (何かあったのかもしれないね). 근거: 음성 「遅おくれてくるはずないんだけどね」 — 늦지 않을 사람인데 늦는 이유 추측. 오답: 1. 정답. 2. 「いつも遅おくれてくる」=평소 늦는다는 모순. 3. 「もう来きてる…早はやい」=벌써 와 있다는 반대. 포인트: 「~はずない」=~할 리 없다 / 「~かもしれない」=~일지도 모른다. 추측 표현.

questions.script_html:
女：すみません、このパンフレット、いただいてもよろしいでしょうか。
1　ぜひ拝見はいけんさせてください。
2　では、いただいておきます。
3　どうぞ、お持もちください。
questions.translation_en:
F: Excuse me, may I take this pamphlet?
1. Please let me take a look at it.
2. Then, I’ll take it with me.
3. Please, go ahead and take it.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (どうぞ、お持ちください). 근거: 음성 「いただいてもよろしいでしょうか」 — 가져가도 되는지 묻는 손님에게 허락. 오답: 1. 「拝見はいけんさせてください」=보게 해 주세요(엉뚱). 2. 「いただいておきます」=받겠습니다(상대 발화). 3. 정답. 포인트: 「お+동사 연용형+ください」=정중 권유. 「お持もちください」=가져가십시오.

questions.script_html:
男：先輩せんぱい、来週らいしゅうのパーティー、ちっとも人ひとが集あつまらなくて。
1　みんな来きたんだね。

3　それは困こまったね。
questions.translation_en:

questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (それは困ったね). 근거: 음성 「ちっとも人ひとが集あつまらなくて」 — 사람이 안 모이는 곤란함에 공감. 오답: 1. 「みんな来きたんだね」=다 왔다는 반대. 2. 「たくさん集あつまってよかった」=반대 상황. 3. 정답. 포인트: 「ちっとも~ない」=조금도 ~않다. 부정 강조.

questions.script_html:
女：吉田よしださんほど親切しんせつな人ひとっていないよね。
1　吉田よしださんって親切しんせつじゃないの。
2　あんな人ひと、なかなかいないと思う。
3　親切しんせつな人ひとがすごく多おおいってこと。
questions.translation_en:
F: There aren’t many people as kind as Yoshida, are there?
1. Isn’t Yoshida kind?
2. I think there aren’t many people like him.
3. It means there are many kind people.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (あんな人、なかなかいないと思う). 근거: 음성 「吉田よしださんほど親切しんせつな人ひとっていないよね」 — 그만큼 친절한 사람 드물다는 칭찬에 동의. 오답: 1. 「親切しんせつじゃないの」=반대 이해. 2. 정답. 3. 「親切しんせつな人ひとがすごく多おおい」=반대 의미. 포인트: 「~ほど~ない」=~만큼 ~하지 않다 / 「なかなか~ない」=좀처럼 ~않다. 최상급 표현.

questions.script_html:
男：池田いけださん、もうすぐお客きゃくさん来くるから、先さきに会議室かいぎしつの暖房だんぼう、つけといて。
1　あっ、少すこし前まえに付つけました。
2　すぐ消けします。すみません。
3　まだ付ついていましたか。
questions.translation_en:
M: Ikeda, the guests will arrive soon, so please turn on the heater in the meeting room beforehand.
1. Oh, I turned it on a little while ago.
2. I’ll turn it off right away. Sorry.
3. Was it still on?
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (あっ、少し前に付けました). 근거: 음성 「先さきに会議室かいぎしつの暖房だんぼう、つけといて」 — 미리 켜 두라는 지시에 이미 켰다고 보고. 오답: 1. 정답. 2. 「すぐ消けします」=끄겠다(반대). 3. 「まだ付ついていましたか」=아직 켜져 있었나(엉뚱). 포인트: 「~ておく」=~해 두다 / 「少すこし前まえに付つけました」=조금 전에 켰습니다. 사전 준비 보고.
